title: OPERATOR DIRECTIVE: graduated remediation — when the implementation is sound and a gate FAIL enumerates MINOR mechanical defects, fix forward in the same cycle (bounded) instead of throwing the whole effort away; terminal FAIL only for core/design/premise/system errors
why: OPERATOR PRIORITIZATION 2026-07-21: token-optimization class bumped by directive ('prioritize and bump token optimization related tasks'). OPERATOR 2026-07-21: 'we should not waste all the effort and throw away work for minor errors — only for implementation/core errors.' MEASURED CASE FILE: cycle-983 coverage-gate FAIL PRESCRIBED the exact missing test (landing_test.go recipe) yet the cycle died and the work re-ran later (~12.5M-token wrapper per retry vs ~2.8M for one fix dispatch); cycle-992 mechanical coverage+type-safety; 3 apicover main-REDs on 2026-07-20 each fixed by a ~40-line naming test. COUNTER-CASES prove the boundary is real: 991 premise-falsified and 1000/1001 SYSTEM-class were correctly terminal. The deliverable-contract seam ALREADY has this pattern (E2 correction ladder: reject -> re-dispatch -> re-review, bounded) — gate/audit verdicts lack it.
how_to_apply: Extend the E2 correction-ladder pattern to gate verdicts, integrity-first: (1) CLASSIFY deterministically — a FAIL is remediable IFF its structured defects are all mechanical classes (coverage-below-floor with prescribed tests, unnamed-export/apicover, lint/format, missing regression test, doc-delta) AND no defect is design/premise/security/system class AND the failing gates are deterministic or defect-scoped; ambiguity => terminal (fail-closed). (2) REMEDIATE once: re-dispatch builder into the SAME preserved worktree with ONLY the defect list + prescriptions (scoped context, not a full re-read), one round (config cap, default 1). (3) SCOPE GUARD: diff between remediation start/end must stay within the defect scope — new files/packages beyond it => terminal FAIL (no smuggling under remediation). (4) RE-VERIFY: re-run ONLY the failed gates natively (coverage/apicover/type-safety are deterministic and ungameable) + the ship floor; if the LLM audit was the failer, re-audit scoped to the defect list with the ORIGINAL audit attached (anti-cooperative-bias: the re-audit must cite each defect resolved). (5) Verdict provenance: cycle records remediated=true + rounds + original defects — never a silent PASS; EGPS and adversarial gates unchanged. (6) Telemetry: count remediation saves vs cost to prove the economics. Complements (does not replace) preserved-worktree salvage, which stays for terminal FAILs.
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-983/coverage-gate-report.md (prescribed exact test); cycle-992 mechanical gate FAILs; apicover incidents 9eacd83f/aaeb4d4d/c37d-class; E2 correction ladder cyclerun_review.go; preserveOnVerdict salvage precedent (975->981 worked but cost a full new cycle); token economics knowledge-base/research/token-usage-history-2026-07-20.md (~7.6M wrapper/cycle).
connects_to: graduated remediation is the per-cycle dual of synthesis: synthesis avoids N cycles for one disease; remediation avoids re-cycling sound work for mechanical defects
